description: Restrictive 'li' says that the following constituent alone satisfies the claim. It can fence a person or thing, and it can bound a time such as 'li nosa' (only now). Focus 'ko' (FOC) singles out an alternative and leaves the others in play.
articulatory_notes: The tongue tip touches the alveolar ridge for 'l' while voiced air passes around its sides. The tongue body then rises into high-front unrounded 'i'; the single syllable is stressed.
sound_symbolism: The lateral airflow finishes in the narrow front vowel 'i'. That compact motion may suggest scope drawing to one choice.
usage_notes: The particle occupies the widest Slot 2 position and never shares a phrase with 'we' (ALSO). It stands immediately before the whole constituent under restriction. Counts state their own quantity; when a count falls short, Phi uses 'henoi ma nai' (there is not enough) instead of attaching 'li' to a numeral.
